data.title: Australia’s inflation surges to 3.8% in December, surpassing expectations
data.description:
Australia's annual inflation rose to 3.8% in December 2025, up from 3.4% in November and exceeding estimates of 3.6%.

Services inflation reached a two-year high of 4.1%, influenced by holiday travel and rising rents, while goods inflation increased slightly to 3.4%.

The trimmed mean CPI inched up to 3.3% y/y from the prior 3.2%, in line with estimates. Monthly, the CPI rose 1.0%, picking up from a flat print in November.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.2% to around 8,960 in Wednesday morning trade, on track for a fourth consecutive winning streak to hit a fresh three-month high. The Australian dollar traded around $0.699 on Wednesday, hovering near a three-year high, after a hotter-than-expected inflation print boosted expectations of a rate hike by the Reserve Bank next month. 

More on Australia:

data.title: BYD reportedly weighs India expansion as hundreds of car orders pile up
data.description:
BYD Co. (BYDDY) (BYDDF) is said to be weighing options to expand in India, including local assembly to meet surging demand for the Chinese automaker’s electric vehicles.

The company is evaluating some form of local assembly in India and working on obtaining local safety and regulatory certifications for more models because of import quotas, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Though India previously rejected BYD's (BYDDY) plans to build a full assembly plant in the country, the Chinese company is considering putting together semi-assembled parts—which would be cheaper and easier to clear in terms of regulatory approvals, the report said.

Any manufacturing move would follow a visit by senior BYD executives.

Strong demand is leading the automaker to reconsider how it can bring more vehicles into the country, sources said, noting that dealers are holding hundreds of customer bookings. This stands in stark contrast to Tesla (TSLA), which has been rolling out discounts on select models to spur sales in India.

data.title: Trump-backed American Bitcoin adds 416 BTC; reserves rise to 5,843
data.description:
• American Bitcoin (ABTC) has increased its total Bitcoin reserve to ~5,843 BTC and achieved a BTC yield of ~116% from its Nasdaq debut on September 3, 2025, through January 25, 2026, the company said.
  • The latest figures place American Bitcoin as the 18th-largest corporate holder of bitcoin, ahead of firms such as Nakamoto Inc (NAKA) and GameStop Corp (GME), CoinDesk reported.
  • American Bitcoin (ABTC) is down 6.5% year to date as investors contend with shifting macroeconomic conditions, geopolitical uncertainty, and a recent slide in bitcoin prices.
  • American Bitcoin is about 20% owned by Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump and became an independent public company after merging with Gryphon Digital Mining and separating from Hut 8’s (HUT) mining business.

data.title: Altimmune prices $75M direct offering
data.description:
• Altimmune (ALT) entered into a securities purchase agreement with a new fundamental institutional investor for the purchase and sale of 17.05M shares pursuant to a registered direct offering.
  • The offering is expected to result in gross proceeds of ~$75M.
  • All the shares and pre-funded warrants in the offering are being offered by Altimmune.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 29, 2026.
  • The net proceeds will be used to fund preparation for its upcoming Phase 3 trial in metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis as well as for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The stock price traded 3% higher on Tuesday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Woodside Energy Group reports Q4 results; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Woodside Energy Group press release (WDS): Q4 Revenue of $3.04B (-12.6% Y/Y).
  • Delivered record full-year production of 198.8 MMboe (545 Mboe/d), exceeding 2025 production guidance.
  • Quarterly production of 48.9 MMboe (531 Mboe/d), down 4% from Q3 2025, driven by seasonal weather impacts and lower Australian east-coast demand.
  • Delivered strong oil asset performance with 99.2% reliability at Sangomar and 98% reliability at Shenzi.
  • Achieved a second consecutive quarter of 100% reliability at Pluto LNG and 99.8% reliability at the North West Shelf Project.
  • Achieved an average realised quarterly price of $57/boe, down 5% from Q3 2025 reflecting lower oil-linked and gas pricing.
  • “We are looking forward to first LNG from Scarborough in the fourth quarter of this year. Our 2026 volume guidance of 172 - 186 MMboe reflects planned down time at Pluto as we prepare the facility to begin processing Scarborough gas and for first LNG cargo in Q4 2026."

data.title: Embraer order backlog hits record $31.6B, up 20% year on year
data.description:
• Embraer, the Brazilian aircraft manufacturer (EMBJ), reported a record firm order backlog of $31.6 billion at the end of the fourth quarter, marking a 20% increase from a year earlier, according to a securities filing released on Tuesday.
  • The company delivered 91 aircraft in the quarter, a 21% increase from the year-ago period. 
  • The stock (EMBJ) advanced 1.1% in extended trading to $80.75 and is up 98% in the past year.

data.title: Amazon reportedly sends premature layoff email to workers fearing big cuts
data.description:
• Amazon.com (AMZN) employees who have been anticipating layoffs numbering in the thousands got one more thing to worry about late Tuesday: a meeting invite and email from a top executive that was sent prematurely, Bloomberg News reported.
  • The email from Amazon Senior Vice President Colleen Aubrey, reviewed by Bloomberg, scheduled a meeting for Wednesday morning that has since been canceled, titled “Project Dawn.”
  • The email said “impacted colleagues” based in the US, Canada, and Costa Rica had been notified and referred to a separate message from Amazon’s human resources chief, Beth Galetti, thatBeth Galetti that does not appear to have been sent.

data.title: Seagate outlines 34% year-over-year revenue growth target for March quarter as HAMR adoption accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Seagate Technology Holdings plc (STX) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO William Mosley announced that Seagate closed calendar 2025 with "a record-breaking quarter, driven by sequential revenue growth across nearly all end markets." Mosley stated, "December quarter financial results exceeded both top and bottom line expectations and set new company records for exabyte shipments, gross margin, operating margin and non-GAAP earnings per share. We expanded non-GAAP gross margin above 42%, supported by the execution of our pricing strategy, along with an improving mix of our high capacity drives as HAMR shipments ramp."
  • Mosley highlighted that "2025 also solidified HAMR technology as a long-term enabler of mass capacity storage," sharing the milestone of shipping "3 terabyte per disk Mozaic-based HAMR products to our first CSP customer" and surpassing "1.5 million units" of quarterly HAMR shipments.
  • Mosley added, "Our nearline capacity is fully allocated through calendar year 2026, and we expect to begin accepting orders for the first half of calendar year 2027 in the coming months."
  • CFO Gianluca Romano stated, "Seagate delivered another quarter of strong year-over-year revenue growth and set new record profitability metrics in the December quarter, underscoring the durability of data center demand trends. Additionally, we strengthened our financial position by retiring $500 million in gross debt and generating over $600 million in free cash flow, marking the highest level in 8 years."

Outlook

  • Romano provided guidance for the March quarter: "We expect March quarter revenue to be in the range of $2.9 billion, plus or minus $100 million, which represent a 34% year-over-year improvement as a midpoint. Non-GAAP operating expenses are expected to be approximately $290 million. Based on the midpoint of our revenue guidance, non-GAAP operating margin is expected to approach the [ mid-30% range ]. Non-GAAP EPS is expected to be $3.40 plus or minus $0.20, based on a tax rate of about 16% and non-GAAP diluted share count of 230 million shares."
  • Management stated, "we expect free cash flow generation to further expand in the March quarter, supported by sustained demand trends, operational efficiency and capital discipline."
  • Sequential improvement in both top and bottom line is expected throughout calendar 2026.

Financial Results

  • Romano reported December quarter revenue of $2.83 billion, a 7% sequential and 22% year-over-year increase. Non-GAAP gross margin reached 42.2%, up 210 basis points sequentially. Non-GAAP operating margin rose 290 basis points sequentially to 31.9%.
  • Non-GAAP EPS was $3.11, up 19% quarter-over-quarter.
  • Data center revenue totaled $2.2 billion for the quarter, a 5% sequential and 28% year-on-year increase.
  • Free cash flow generation was $607 million, up 42% from the prior quarter. Gross debt balance was approximately $4.5 billion at quarter end.
  • Net leverage ratio improved to 1.1x based on adjusted EBITDA of $962 million.

Q&A

  • Christopher Muse, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about gross margin outlook and potential for year-over-year pricing improvement. Mosley responded, "pricing will be dictated by the demand. Right now, the demand is really strong... flat to slightly up is certainly possible. And that's the way we're really managing it as we talk to our customers."
  • Wamsi Mohan, BofA Securities: Inquired about drivers of gross margin improvement. CFO Romano stated, "we are executing very well, but executing a little bit better than what we discussed at our Investor Day... our objective to continue to optimize what we produce, what we sell and finance the profitability that we can get from the product."
  • Erik Woodring, Morgan Stanley: Asked about exabyte supply growth pace. Mosley explained, "we are planning to transition to 4 terabytes of platter... It's a good problem to have, actually. We're running manufacturing quite, quite tight right now."
  • Asiya Merchant, Citigroup: Sought detail on HAMR rollout and cost reductions. Romano said, "transition from PMR technology to HAMR technology, is progressing very well... the cost will be favorably impacted, especially when we start ramping high volume of the 40 terabyte drive."
  • Multiple analysts probed on pricing dynamics for long-term agreements (LTAs), HAMR mix, and capital expenditures. Management maintained that supply for 2026 is largely committed, with pricing for 2027 still under negotiation.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on gross margin drivers, pricing flexibility, and supply-demand tightness, reflecting a slightly positive to neutral tone but with ongoing curiosity about sustainability of current trends.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A. Mosley and Romano used assertive language such as "we are very happy," "we are executing very well," and "we expect both revenue and profitability to improve sequentially."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts' tone showed increased conviction, with less uncertainty around technology transitions and financial guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue, gross margin, and operating margin all increased sequentially, with non-GAAP gross margin rising from 40.1% to 42.2% and operating margin from 29% to 31.9%.
  • The March quarter guidance projects a considerably higher year-over-year revenue growth rate than the previous quarter's outlook.
  • Management's confidence in HAMR technology and demand visibility into 2027 has strengthened.
  • Analysts' questions shifted towards deeper pricing dynamics, ramp speed of new technologies, and long-term agreement structures, indicating growing interest in sustainability of high margins and capacity allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing supply-demand tightness but indicated supply is fully allocated for 2026, reducing near-term risk of underutilization.
  • Capital discipline remains a focus, with CapEx guided to remain inside the 4% to 6% range of revenue.
  • No significant changes in manufacturing strategy or risk of rapid capacity expansion were discussed, but the pace of HAMR qualification and yield ramp remains a factor to monitor.

Final Takeaway

Seagate management emphasized that 2025 was a milestone year, marked by record financial performance and the successful ramp of HAMR technology. With capacity fully allocated through 2026 and robust demand from data center customers, the company projects continued revenue and profitability improvements throughout 2026. The ongoing transition to higher-capacity HAMR drives is expected to enhance margins and cost efficiency, supporting Seagate's long-term value proposition for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Logitech outlines Q4 net sales growth target of 3%-5% with strong B2B momentum amid global expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Logitech International S.A. (LOGI) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Johanna Faber reported another period of strong financial performance, highlighting "record non-GAAP operating income and earnings per share" outside of pandemic peaks. Faber described "very strong non-GAAP gross margins" and "top line growth of plus 6% in U.S. dollars and 4% in constant currency" as broad-based across regions, channels, and categories. She emphasized the launch of the MX Master 4 mouse, which "sold more units in the first month following launch than any other personal workspace mouse in Logitech's history." Faber also noted strong demand for the Pro X Superlight 2 mouse and the G3116 gaming keyboard in China, which contributed to market share gains there. She stated, "AI now plays a pretty critical role when it comes to superior video and audio innovation... We are shipping AI products globally at scale." B2B demand "significantly outpaced B2C demand in the third quarter," especially in video collaboration and education. Faber highlighted manufacturing diversification, stating the company "successfully reduced the percentage of U.S. products manufactured in China from 40% last April to less than 10% by the end of December 2025."
  • CFO Matteo Anversa stated, "Non-GAAP operating income reached $312 million, reflecting a 17% year-over-year increase alongside a 220 basis point expansion in profitability." He reported "net sales were $1.4 billion, up 4% year-over-year in constant currency," with growth being the "eighth quarter of consecutive top line growth." Anversa highlighted "exceptional cash flow generation of approximately $500 million—a 30% year-over-year increase." He also mentioned, "cash conversion cycle improved by 18% down to a highly efficient 27 days" and a quarter-ending "cash balance of $1.8 billion."

Outlook

  • Management expects Q4 net sales to grow 3% to 5% year-over-year in constant currency, with a gross margin rate of approximately 43% to 44%, and non-GAAP operating income between $155 million and $165 million. CFO Anversa said, "Net sales in the fourth quarter are expected to grow 3% to 5% year-over-year in constant currency with a gross margin rate of approximately 43% to 44%, and non-GAAP operating income is expected to be between $155 million and $165 million, up 20% year-over-year at the midpoint."
  • Management stated, "We expect to close fiscal year '26 above the long-term model targets for non-GAAP gross margin and non-GAAP operating margin that we outlined at our Analyst and Investor Day last year."

Financial Results

  • Logitech reported non-GAAP operating income of $312 million, a 17% year-over-year increase, and a non-GAAP gross margin of 43.5%. Net sales reached $1.4 billion, marking the eighth consecutive quarter of top line growth. Personal workspace net sales increased 7%, with pointing devices up 9% and double-digit growth in tablet accessories. Video collaboration net sales grew 8%, while gaming net sales increased 2%, driven by double-digit growth in Asia Pacific. EMEA saw 2% growth, and Americas returned to modest growth. Operating expenses were $306 million, down 2% year-over-year. The company ended the quarter with a cash balance of $1.8 billion and generated $500 million in operating cash flow.

Q&A

  • Asiya Merchant, Citi: Asked about confidence in long-term growth, the impact of memory availability, and sustainability of gross margins. CEO Faber responded that "the vast majority of our portfolio is not impacted by the current tight memory availability" and expects no supply impact in Q4 or the first half of the next fiscal year. Faber emphasized, "our peripherals business...continues to have excellent growth opportunities, whatever the environment." CFO Anversa added, "the foundation of this gross margin and our ability to maintain the gross margin to this level...is there."
  • Joern Iffert, UBS: Inquired about attachment rates for mice and keyboards compared to 5-10 years ago and concerns about gaming market declines in the U.S. and Europe. CEO Faber reported, "In the U.S. and Europe, we held share in a declining market...our U.S. share stabilize after a couple of quarters." She noted, "We're winning at the top end with Pro and at the entry level," particularly in China. CFO Anversa noted that "peripherals outpace PC sales by about 300 to 500 basis points on average," with current attach rates between 9% and 14%.
  • Erik Woodring, Morgan Stanley: Sought clarification on PC sales unit versus revenue outperformance and how Logitech plans to drive peripheral adoption. CFO Anversa confirmed the outperformance is based on unit sales, and CEO Faber cited product superiority and targeted marketing as key drivers.
  • Ananda Baruah, Loop Capital: Asked if current growth could outpace historical averages due to PC dynamics and if pull-forward sales were occurring. CEO Faber said it's too early to speculate but highlighted the company's resilience. CFO Anversa and Faber both indicated they "have not seen or heard of any pull forwards in our business."
  • Joseph Cardoso, JPMorgan: Queried further reduction of U.S. manufacturing from China and margin implications. CFO Anversa stated, "At this point, I think we are happy where we are" regarding the China share and is "happy with the current landscape."
  • Didier Scemama, BofA: Asked about volume vs. price in personal workspace growth and pricing strategy in a tough PC market. CEO Faber described Q3 growth as "a combination of all 3"—units, premiumization, and pricing. "We're very intentional and strategic on when we need to promote on certain parts of the portfolio."
  • Unknown Analyst, BMP: Sought clarity on Q4 guidance swing factors and promotional activity. CFO Anversa explained that "the swing factor is...AMR" and that "sell-through was up 10% year-over-year in the third quarter," with slightly higher promotional spend into the holiday season.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts asked probing questions about sustainability of growth, impact of memory shortages, and market share concerns in gaming, indicating a neutral to slightly cautious tone.
  • Management's tone was confident in prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly referencing resilience and operational execution. CEO Faber stated, "This is a company for all seasons," and CFO Anversa said, "the foundation and the execution of the team is there."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management sustained a confident tone, while analysts remained focused on risks and near-term uncertainties, particularly around market share and macroeconomic headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 saw continued strong net sales and margin performance, with incremental improvements in Americas growth and operational execution. The previous quarter emphasized uncertainty in the U.S. consumer market, especially gaming, while the current quarter highlighted broad-based growth and stabilization in U.S. market share.
  • Guidance language shifted from "pragmatic balance" in Q2 to a more assertive outlook in Q3, with management expecting to "close fiscal year '26 above the long-term model targets." Strategic focus on B2B and manufacturing diversification deepened, with China exposure in U.S. manufacturing dropping below 10%.
  • Analyst questions in both quarters focused on gaming market dynamics, pricing impacts, and sustainability of margin improvements, but Q3 included more inquiries about AI products and the installed PC base opportunity.
  • Management maintained a high level of confidence in both quarters, but Q3 remarks were more detailed on execution and supply chain resilience.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified ongoing monitoring of external dynamics, including geopolitics, tariffs, and consumer confidence as key risks. Faber and Anversa both explicitly referenced these factors.
  • Memory availability was discussed as a potential challenge, but management stated they do not foresee a supply impact in the coming quarters, though there may be "a modest cost impact."
  • Analysts raised concerns about U.S. and European gaming market declines, the sustainability of gross margin improvements, and the effects of pricing strategies in a potentially contracting PC market. Management responded with explanations of proactive mitigation strategies and emphasized cost discipline.

Final Takeaway

Logitech delivered another strong quarter driven by record non-GAAP operating income, robust cash flow, and continued gross margin expansion, supported by broad-based top-line growth and strategic product launches. Management expressed confidence in meeting or exceeding long-term model targets and highlighted the success of B2B initiatives, global manufacturing diversification, and operational discipline. The company projects Q4 net sales growth of 3% to 5% and expects to close the fiscal year above prior margin targets, positioning itself as resilient amid ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Manhattan Associates targets 21% cloud revenue growth and $2.6B–$2.68B RPO for 2026 as AI and services ramp
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Manhattan Associates (MANH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Eric Clark, President and CEO, announced "record cloud bookings in the fourth quarter" and stated that Manhattan achieved annual records in RPO, cloud bookings, total revenue, operating income, free cash flow, and earnings per share. He highlighted new product innovations, including the commercial launch of AI agents and Agent Foundry, which enables customers to build or customize agents in the Active platform. Clark emphasized, "results and feedback from our early adopters indicate that our AI agent workforce generates significant value as increased automation and simplicity can drive higher productivity, ROI and improve customer satisfaction."
  • Clark detailed organizational changes, including the reorganization of the global sales team under Chief Sales Officer Bob Howell, the hiring of Chief Operating Officer Greg Betz, and the addition of Chief Marketing Officer Katie Foote, stating, "we reorganized our entire global sales team...added several new sales leaders and product specialists...and hired Greg Betz as our Chief Operating Officer."
  • Clark provided a vertical update, listing Q4 wins across several industries, and highlighted that more than 75% of new cloud bookings in Q4 came from net new logos, while new logos represented over 55% of annual new cloud bookings.
  • CFO Dennis Story stated, "in 2025, we set records across bookings, our P&L and cash flow." Story introduced a new 4-year annualized value of recurring revenue (ramped ARR) metric, reporting ramped ARR exceeded $600 million, up 23% year-over-year. He also noted, "Q4 cloud revenue totaled $109 million, up 20%, and includes a customer liquidation headwind of $1.3 million that was not embedded in our guidance."

Outlook

  • For 2026, management targets RPO of $2.62 billion to $2.68 billion, representing 18% to 20% growth, with 18% to 20% of the RPO mix from renewals. Full-year total revenue is expected between $1.133 billion and $1.153 billion, with the midpoint representing 10% growth excluding license and maintenance attrition and 6% all-in. Cloud revenue is projected to increase 21% to $492 million, while services revenue is forecast to rise 3% to $517 million. Maintenance revenue is expected to decline 19% to $105.5 million. Adjusted EPS guidance is set at a range of $5.04 to $5.20. Story stated, "we expect cloud revenue to increase 21% to $492 million."
  • Clark emphasized, "our pipeline remains strong across our product suite, and we have numerous opportunities to drive growth, including adding new customers, cross-selling our growing unified product portfolio and converting our on-premise customers to the cloud."

Financial Results

  • Q4 total revenue was $270 million, up 6%. Full-year revenue reached $1.08 billion, up 4%. Excluding license and maintenance revenue, Q4 revenue growth was 9% and full year, 5%. Cloud revenue for Q4 was $109 million, and full-year cloud revenue reached $408 million, up 21%.
  • RPO at year-end was $2.2 billion, up 25% year-over-year. Q4 adjusted operating profit was $91 million with a 33.8% margin. Full-year adjusted operating profit was $387 million, with a margin of 35.8%. Adjusted EPS for Q4 was $1.21, and GAAP EPS was $0.86. Full-year adjusted EPS was $5.06, and GAAP EPS was $3.60.
  • Operating cash flow for Q4 increased 40% to $147 million. The company ended the year with $329 million in cash and no debt. Share repurchases for the year totaled $275 million.

Q&A

  • Terrell Tillman, Truist Securities: Asked about progress in cloud migrations and fast renewals. Clark responded that the company is "seeing the pipeline really start to build" and has already closed some deals in Q1, aided by adding 100 services headcount in January. He noted, "we're able to sell more deals at a faster pace."
  • Brian Peterson, Raymond James: Inquired about the drivers of RPO strength. Clark replied, "it really comes across a variety of products and a variety of deal types," and emphasized the introduction of ramped ARR as a complementary metric to RPO.
  • George Kurosawa, Citi: Questioned the 18%-20% renewal mix and services confidence. Clark confirmed the renewal mix and attributed services confidence to strong bookings, conversion programs, and Agentic AI, stating, "we have the domain expertise."
  • Joseph Vruwink, Baird: Asked about new bookings and expansion. Clark highlighted opportunities in cross-sell and expansion, considering it market share gain.
  • Dylan Becker, William Blair: Queried the impact of new initiatives. Clark said most pipeline closed was due to previous groundwork, but new programs set up for 2026 are expected to show results.
  • J. Lane, Stifel: Sought details on AI agent monetization. Clark described a "90-day proof of concept" approach with uplifts upon conversion, aiming for simple pricing and customer adoption.
  • Guy Hardwick, Barclays: Asked if AI agent bookings are included in guidance. Clark replied, "Anything we do in AI is incremental to what we've talked about today."
  • Mark Schappel, Loop Capital: Requested commentary on CIO sentiment and deal mix. Clark observed "very healthy pipeline" and a "remarkable performance" in new logos, suggesting the 1/3 split between new logo, expansion, and conversion will normalize over time.
  • Clark Wright, DA Davidson: Asked about post-cloud conversion upsell drivers. Clark explained ongoing quarterly updates and steady incremental services.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive to slightly positive tone, focusing on growth drivers, renewals, and AI monetization while probing for detail on guidance conservatism and pipeline visibility.
  • Management’s sentiment was confident throughout, with Clark repeatedly highlighting momentum, pipeline strength, and organizational changes. Phrases like "we're very bullish in that area" and "we're confident" supported a strong tone, both in prepared remarks and Q&A.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained strong confidence, but management introduced more detailed metrics and product-specific excitement, especially around AI and new services.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is more specific, with explicit RPO, revenue, and cloud growth targets, compared to broader preliminary commentary in Q3. Management introduced ramped ARR as a new metric for visibility.
  • More detail on AI commercialization and monetization strategy was provided this quarter, with actual product launches and early customer feedback, whereas Q3 focused on early access and pipeline building.
  • Q4 showed a sharper organizational focus with notable hires and sales team restructuring, building on the COO appointment announced in Q3.
  • The services business outlook shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to more actionable confidence, supported by headcount increases and visible backlog.
  • Analysts shifted from questions on overall execution and macro uncertainty in Q3 to specifics on growth composition and AI adoption in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged a "volatile macro environment" and customer liquidation headwinds, noting a $1.3 million cloud revenue impact in Q4 and $2.5 million annualized effect for 2026.
  • License and maintenance attrition is expected to be a 4-point headwind to overall revenue growth in 2026, and maintenance revenue is forecast to decline by 19%.
  • Seasonal lumpiness in bookings and the timing of large deals continue to pose risks to quarterly performance.
  • Management’s guidance assumes no churn or price increases in the ramped ARR metric, which could be conservative if market conditions shift.

Final Takeaway

Management closed the call by emphasizing a strong finish to 2025 and a confident outlook for 2026, driven by record cloud bookings, robust new logo momentum, and the commercial launch of AI products. The company projects double-digit growth in key cloud metrics, expanded services capacity, and continued organizational strengthening, positioning Manhattan Associates for sustained, visible growth in a dynamic market environment.
